title: Labyrinthulomycete total RNA extraction
author: Jackie Collier
materials: Allow the pellets to air dry

steps.title: Grow up cells, collect, and freeze...
steps.instruction: Grow up cells, collect, and freeze rapidly - preferably in liquid nitrogen. Store biomass at -80C if not extracting immediately. This protocol has worked so far for two different thraustochytrids ( Aurantiochytrium limacinum ATCC MYA-1381, Schizochytrium aggregatum ATCC 28920) and two Aplanochytrium strains (PBS06 and PBS07).

steps.title: Prepare extraction buffer 100 mM Tris-HCl pH 7
steps.instruction: Prepare extraction buffer 100 mM Tris-HCl pH 7.5 1.5 M NaCl 50 mM Na 2 -EDTA pH 8.0 20 g per liter CTAB (cetyltrimethylammonium bromide) 8 mg per ml DTT (dithiothreitol) - ADD FRESH JUST BEFORE USE (note, if you keep these stocks in glass bottles, first soak the bottles with 0.1 M NaOH to inactivate RNAses) Prepare Tris-equilibrated phenol, pH 4.5-4.8, by warming to 65 C Prepare acid phenol:chloroform:isoamyl alcohol (pH 4.5-4.8, 125:24:1) by warming to 65 C Set up to incubate the extraction step at 65 C Prepare 8M LiCl Chill 100% ethanol and 70% ethanol Prepare TE or nuclease-free water Get a bucket of ice

steps.title: Suspend ~1000 micrograms wet weight biomass...
steps.instruction: Suspend ~1000 micrograms wet weight biomass per ~10 ml extraction buffer by vortexing (these amounts would yield tens of micrograms of total RNA)

steps.title: Add an equal volume of isopropanol...
steps.instruction: Add an equal volume of isopropanol and incubate overnight at 4 C

steps.title: Incubate at 65 C for 5...
steps.instruction: Incubate at 65 C for 5 minutes, mix by vortex or inverting 2 or 3 times

steps.title: Add equal volume hot (65 C)...
steps.instruction: Add equal volume hot (65 C) acid phenol/chloroform/isoamyl alcohol, votes to mix well

steps.title: Incubate at 65 C for 5...
steps.instruction: Incubate at 65 C for 5 minutes, mix by vortex or inverting 2 or 3 times

steps.title: Cool the mixture in ice
steps.instruction: Cool the mixture in ice

steps.title: Centrifuge to separate the phases 4 C, 6,000g, 15 minutes
steps.instruction: Centrifuge to separate the phases 4 C, 6,000g, 15 minutes

steps.title: Move the top phase to a...
steps.instruction: Move the top phase to a new tube, avoiding the interface

steps.title: If remaining DNA forms a large...
steps.instruction: If remaining DNA forms a large fluffy precipitate, remove by spooling onto glass rod

steps.title: Collect precipitate by centrifugation 4 C,...
steps.instruction: Collect precipitate by centrifugation 4 C, 16,000g, 30 minutes (may require distributing the sample among several 1.5 ml microfuge tubes and/or multiple spins for each tube to process the volume)

steps.title: Wash each pellet with 0
steps.instruction: Wash each pellet with 0.5 ml ice-cold 75% ethanol twice, centrifuging 4 C 16,000g, 5 minutes

steps.title: Dissolve pellets in an appropriate volume,...
steps.instruction: Dissolve pellets in an appropriate volume, analyze by appropriate method (spectrophotometry, Bioanalyzer); store frozen at -80 C

steps.title: If the RNA contains particulates that...
steps.instruction: If the RNA contains particulates that do not dissolve, they can be removed by centrifugation 4 C, 6,000g, 5 minutes and transferring the supernatant to a fresh tube.

steps.title: If the RNA contains large amounts...
steps.instruction: If the RNA contains large amounts of DNA or other contaminants, it can be removed by selectively precipitating the RNA with 17 microliters of 8M LiCl per 50 microliters RNA

steps.title: Incubate -20 C 60 minutes
steps.instruction: Incubate -20 C 60 minutes

steps.title: Collect RNA by centrifugation 4 C, 16,000g, 20 minutes
steps.instruction: Collect RNA by centrifugation 4 C, 16,000g, 20 minutes

steps.title: Wash each pellet with 0
steps.instruction: Wash each pellet with 0.5 ml ice-cold 75% ethanol twice, centrifuging 4 C 16,000g, 5 minutes

steps.title: Dissolve pellets in an appropriate volume,...
steps.instruction: Dissolve pellets in an appropriate volume, analyze by appropriate method (spectrophotometry, Bioanalyzer); store frozen at -80 C

steps.title: Even if the RNA does not...
steps.instruction: Even if the RNA does not have obvious DNA, a DNAse treatment step is nonetheless recommended
